title: Julia perturbation rendering (delta iteration with varying seed, dc=0)
Single-pass rendering pipeline
description:
## Why this feature exists

The current render flow is split into two actions:
1) fractal compute (parallel) -> Vec<u32>
2) colour mapping (single-threaded) -> PixelBuffer

That architecture is easy to reason about, but it is now a performance and memory bottleneck in the common render path. We pay for a full-frame intermediate allocation, a second complete traversal, and serial colour mapping. This feature replaces the default controller pipeline with a single-pass renderer that computes iteration values and maps colours within the same parallel row work.

## Product-level goals and constraints

Primary goals:
- Eliminate full-frame intermediate Vec<u32> allocation in the common pipeline used by CLI and interactive rendering.
- Parallelize colour mapping work (remove serial second pass bottleneck).
- Preserve output parity: resulting PixelBuffer must be byte-for-byte identical to the previous two-stage pipeline for the same algorithm, viewport, and colour map.
- Preserve interactive cancellation contract: cancellation must surface as RenderOutcome::Cancelled (not an error string).
- Preserve core architecture constraints: do not break FractalAlgorithm object-safety in this iteration.

Secondary goals:
- Keep old lower-level actions available unless clearly dead or unneeded.
- Add benchmark coverage so speedup is measurable and reproducible.
- Make this body of work fully self-documenting for future maintainers.

## Scope boundaries

In scope:
- Core API changes required for parallel error propagation (ColourMapError Send+Sync).
- New render_pixel_buffer action module with cancelable and non-cancelable rayon entry points.
- Controller integration (CLI and interactive).
- Benchmark updates for apples-to-apples comparison.
- Comprehensive unit and integration-style tests for correctness, error propagation, and cancellation semantics.
- Documentation refresh for architecture and pipeline references.

Out of scope for this feature (tracked separately as follow-up):
- Redesigning FractalAlgorithm trait/object model for true per-pixel interleaving with zero row-local temporary vectors.
- Optional non-rayon serial single-pass backend unless demanded by runtime or platform needs.

## Dependency-driven execution plan

This feature intentionally uses dependency layering to reduce risk:
1. fractal_explorer-hsb.1 updates colour-map error typing first.
2. fractal_explorer-hsb.2 implements the core single-pass action.
3. fractal_explorer-hsb.6 lands correctness, error, and cancellation tests as a confidence gate.
4. fractal_explorer-hsb.3 and fractal_explorer-hsb.4 switch CLI and interactive controllers after test coverage exists.
5. fractal_explorer-hsb.5 adds benchmark comparisons.
6. fractal_explorer-hsb.7 performs docs and cleanup alignment.
7. fractal_explorer-hsb.8 captures non-day1 zero-intermediate exploration direction as deferred work.
8. fractal_explorer-hsb.9 captures optional serial-backend exploration as deferred work.

## Notes for future self

- The design intentionally keeps FractalAlgorithm::compute_row_segment_into unchanged to preserve current optimized coordinate stepping and trait-object usage.
- The single-pass implementation should remain deterministic despite rayon scheduling because each row writes to an exclusive fixed slice.
- Cancellation remains cooperative and should be checked at row boundaries and per configured segment interval.
- The non-cancelable API should delegate to the cancelable implementation using NeverCancel to avoid dual logic paths.
acceptance_criteria:
1. New single-pass renderer exists in `src/core/actions/render_pixel_buffer/` with both cancelable and non-cancelable APIs and explicit error enums.
2. Common controller flows (CLI and interactive) use single-pass rendering instead of compute-then-map two-stage composition.
3. Behaviour is verified as byte-identical to the previous pipeline for representative scenarios.
4. Interactive cancellation still resolves to `RenderOutcome::Cancelled` and never degrades into user-facing error text.
5. Comprehensive tests cover deterministic output, algorithm failure propagation, colour-map failure propagation, and cancellation behavior.
6. Benchmarks include both `generate_and_map` baseline and `single_pass` variant under identical scenario inputs.
7. Documentation (`ARCHITECTURE.md`, plus pipeline references in `CLAUDE.md` if present) reflects the new default architecture.
8. Follow-up optimization ideas that are intentionally deferred are recorded as explicit beads, not tribal memory.
notes:
Dependency policy: tests are treated as a confidence gate before controller migration closes. This is stricter than the original prose "before or alongside" wording, but intentionally chosen to reduce regressions in cancellation/error handling at the controller boundary.

Quality gate policy: do not consider this feature complete until `cargo test` passes and benchmark harness compiles/runs with both pipeline variants.
close_reason: All 7 steps complete: ColourMapError migration, single-pass renderer, CLI+interactive controller migration, benchmarks, tests, docs

title: Step 1: Update ColourMap error type to Send+Sync for parallel use
description:
## Objective

Enable colour-map failures to travel safely through rayon workers by making the ColourMap error type Send + Sync.

## Why this step exists

The single-pass renderer executes colour mapping inside parallel row workers. Parallel iterator short-circuit paths require error payloads that are thread-safe. The current Box dyn Error return type is not sufficient for this.

## Work breakdown

- Subtask fractal_explorer-hsb.1.1
  Define ColourMapError alias and update the ColourMap trait signature in the shared port.
- Subtask fractal_explorer-hsb.1.2
  Update all Mandelbrot and Julia colour map implementations and boxed delegators to use the new error alias.
- Subtask fractal_explorer-hsb.1.3
  Update generate_pixel_buffer error enums, error source chaining, and downcast-oriented tests.

## Key considerations

- This is a contract tightening change, so any future colour map implementation must return thread-safe errors.
- Keep error meaning unchanged for callers; this is a transport-safety change, not a user-facing behavior change.
- Preserve testability by keeping concrete error types downcastable after boxing.

## Quality gate

Run cargo test and verify no error-propagation regressions in existing mapping tests.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Shared ColourMap port defines ColourMapError as Box dyn Error plus Send + Sync + static.
2. ColourMap trait map method returns Result with ColourMapError.
3. All current Mandelbrot and Julia colour map implementations compile with the new trait signature.
4. generate_pixel_buffer error enums wrap ColourMapError instead of non-thread-safe error objects.
5. Existing downcast and error-source tests remain green.
notes: Ordering intent: this is a hard prerequisite for all single-pass parallel mapping work and should remain fully complete before renderer module implementation proceeds.
close_reason: All subtasks complete — ColourMapError alias introduced, all implementations and error enums migrated

title: Subtask: Introduce ColourMapError alias and trait signature update
description:
Update src/core/actions/generate_pixel_buffer/ports/colour_map.rs to:
- define ColourMapError = Box<dyn std::error::Error + Send + Sync + 'static>
- change ColourMap::map signature to return Result<Colour, ColourMapError>

Document (in issue notes or local code comments where helpful) that this change is required so colour-map failures can safely propagate from rayon worker threads.
close_reason: Introduced ColourMapError alias and updated trait + all implementations

title: Subtask: Migrate Mandelbrot and Julia colour map implementations
description: Update every concrete colour map implementation and boxed delegator so map returns ColourMapError. Ensure both Mandelbrot and Julia colour map families compile with the narrowed error type.
close_reason: Already completed as part of hsb.1.1 — all implementations updated

title: Subtask: Update generate_pixel_buffer error enums and downcast coverage
description: Replace Box<dyn Error> with ColourMapError in generate_pixel_buffer error enums, Display/Error source chains, and tests. Confirm downcast-based assertions still work for concrete colour map error types.
close_reason: Already completed — error enums updated, all 230 tests pass including downcast assertions

title: Step 2: Implement single-pass render_pixel_buffer action module
description:
## Objective

Introduce a new core action module that renders PixelBuffer output in one parallel pass by combining fractal computation and colour mapping.

## Why this step exists

This is the structural center of the feature. Without this module, controllers cannot switch away from the two-stage generate-then-map pipeline.

## Work breakdown

- Subtask fractal_explorer-hsb.2.1
  Create module files, exports, and explicit error enums for cancelable and non-cancelable APIs.
- Subtask fractal_explorer-hsb.2.2
  Implement row-parallel output buffer writing using par_chunks_mut and segment-based algorithm calls.
- Subtask fractal_explorer-hsb.2.3
  Implement cooperative cancellation checks and non-cancelable delegation through NeverCancel.

## Design constraints carried from the plan

- Keep FractalAlgorithm trait shape unchanged for object-safety and existing optimized row-segment code paths.
- Constrain this first iteration to algorithm success type u32 to match current fractal families.
- Preserve deterministic output ordering by assigning each row a fixed output slice.
- Keep cancellation a control-flow outcome, not a user-facing error string.

## Quality gate

The new module compiles, exposes both APIs, and passes test coverage around error surfaces and cancellation behavior.
acceptance_criteria:
1. src/core/actions/render_pixel_buffer module exists and is exported through src/core/actions/mod.rs.
2. Public APIs include both cancelable and non-cancelable rayon entry points.
3. Error enums clearly separate Algorithm, ColourMap, PixelBuffer, and Cancelled paths where applicable.
4. Implementation writes directly into final RGBA byte storage without full-frame intermediate iteration buffer allocation.
5. Cancellation checks occur at row boundaries and within segment loops.
6. Non-cancelable API delegates to cancelable implementation via NeverCancel and maps unreachable cancellation branch safely.
notes: Dependency note: this step is blocked by completion of Step 1 plus its lowest-level compatibility subtask (fractal_explorer-hsb.1.3) so compile-time error typing is stabilized first.
close_reason: Full single-pass renderer implemented with error API, parallel rows, and cancellation

title: Subtask: Create render_pixel_buffer module structure and error API
description: Add src/core/actions/render_pixel_buffer module files, export wiring, and explicit RenderPixelBufferError / RenderPixelBufferCancelableError enums with Display and Error implementations.
close_reason: Full single-pass renderer implemented with error API, parallel rows, and cancellation

title: Subtask: Implement parallel row renderer writing directly to RGBA buffer
description: Implement rayon row parallelism using par_chunks_mut over final pixel buffer bytes, segment by cancel interval, invoke compute_row_segment_into, map iterations to colours, and write deterministic RGBA output.
close_reason: Full single-pass renderer implemented with error API, parallel rows, and cancellation

title: Subtask: Implement cancelable wrapper and NeverCancel delegation
description: Implement cancelable entry point with cooperative cancellation checks at row and segment boundaries. Have non-cancelable API delegate through NeverCancel and map unreachable Cancelled branch into non-cancelable error enum.
close_reason: Full single-pass renderer implemented with error API, parallel rows, and cancellation

title: Step 3: Switch CLI controller to single-pass renderer
description:
## Objective

Switch the CLI test controller to call the new single-pass renderer directly.

## Why this step exists

CLI is the simplest consumer and serves as a low-risk migration target before interactive complexity. It also validates that the default non-cancelable path works end-to-end.

## Work breakdown

- Subtask fractal_explorer-hsb.3.1
  Replace two-stage action calls and update imports in src/controllers/cli/test/cli_test.rs.
- Subtask fractal_explorer-hsb.3.2
  Validate command behavior and output parity for output/mandelbrot.ppm.

## Behavior requirements

- Keep method signatures and external CLI usage unchanged.
- Continue returning Result with boxed std error at controller boundary.
- Preserve generated image bytes for equivalent algorithm and colour map settings.

## Quality gate

cargo test and cargo run succeed, and generated output remains behaviorally equivalent to baseline expectations.
acceptance_criteria:
1. CLI controller no longer composes generate_fractal plus generate_pixel_buffer in its main render path.
2. CLI controller now calls render_pixel_buffer_parallel_rayon directly.
3. Imports and error propagation remain clean and compile without adapter hacks.
4. cargo run still writes output/mandelbrot.ppm and output remains equivalent to prior pipeline.
notes: Risk control: this step is blocked on action-level test matrix readiness (fractal_explorer-hsb.6.4) in addition to renderer completion, so migration is not done blind.
close_reason: CLI and interactive controllers migrated to single-pass renderer, all 240 tests pass, CLI output verified

title: Subtask: Replace CLI two-stage pipeline call site
description: In src/controllers/cli/test/cli_test.rs swap generate_fractal + generate_pixel_buffer sequence for render_pixel_buffer_parallel_rayon and update imports accordingly.
close_reason: CLI and interactive controllers migrated to single-pass renderer, all 240 tests pass, CLI output verified

title: Subtask: Validate CLI output parity and command path
description:
Validate CLI migration quality after single-pass switch.

Validation checklist:
1. Run cargo test to ensure no regression in unit coverage.
2. Run cargo run and confirm output/mandelbrot.ppm is generated.
3. Verify output parity against expected behavior baseline (either prior reference artifact or equivalence tests) so migration does not alter visual data unexpectedly.
4. Confirm no controller-level errors are surfaced during standard generation path.

Record key command results in implementation notes for future traceability.
acceptance_criteria:
1. cargo test passes after CLI migration changes.
2. cargo run emits output/mandelbrot.ppm successfully.
3. Output parity check is documented and passes.
close_reason: CLI and interactive controllers migrated to single-pass renderer, all 240 tests pass, CLI output verified

title: Step 4: Switch interactive controller to single-pass renderer
description:
## Objective

Switch interactive rendering to the cancelable single-pass action while preserving cancellation semantics and user-facing behavior.

## Why this step exists

Interactive mode has stricter correctness constraints than CLI because stale requests must be cancelled cleanly and silently while fresh requests continue rendering.

## Work breakdown

- Subtask fractal_explorer-hsb.4.1
  Replace two-stage cancelable rendering flow in render_request with single-pass cancelable action call.
- Subtask fractal_explorer-hsb.4.2
  Map error variants to RenderOutcome exactly so cancellation remains non-error control flow.
- Subtask fractal_explorer-hsb.4.3
  Run interactive smoke checks for responsiveness, pan/zoom behavior, and cancellation timing.

## Behavior requirements

- Cancelled work must continue to surface as RenderOutcome::Cancelled only.
- Algorithm, colour-map, and pixel buffer failures should map to RenderOutcome::Error string payloads.
- Generation-id coalescing behavior must remain intact.

## Quality gate

cargo test passes and GUI smoke validation confirms rendering + cancellation behavior remains correct.
acceptance_criteria:
1. render_request no longer performs explicit stage-1 then stage-2 action orchestration.
2. render_request invokes render_pixel_buffer_parallel_rayon_cancelable for full rendering path.
3. RenderPixelBufferCancelableError mapping preserves cancellation as RenderOutcome::Cancelled.
4. No cancellation path leaks into user-facing error outcome text.
5. Interactive flow remains responsive under rapid request replacement.
notes: This step is blocked by action-level tests (fractal_explorer-hsb.6.4) before integration, and controller-contract tests are captured as dedicated subtask fractal_explorer-hsb.6.5.
close_reason: CLI and interactive controllers migrated to single-pass renderer, all 240 tests pass, CLI output verified

title: Subtask: Replace interactive render_request pipeline with single-pass action
description: In src/controllers/interactive/controller.rs remove two-stage cancelable calls and replace them with render_pixel_buffer_parallel_rayon_cancelable invocation.
close_reason: CLI and interactive controllers migrated to single-pass renderer, all 240 tests pass, CLI output verified

title: Subtask: Preserve RenderOutcome cancellation and error translation
description: Map RenderPixelBufferCancelableError variants to RenderOutcome exactly: Cancelled remains control flow, algorithm/colour/pixel-buffer failures become RenderOutcome::Error strings.
close_reason: CLI and interactive controllers migrated to single-pass renderer, all 240 tests pass, CLI output verified

title: Subtask: Interactive smoke validation for pan/zoom and cancellation responsiveness
description:
Run interactive smoke validation after migration using the project GUI testing guidance.

Validation checklist:
1. Build and run GUI with cargo run --bin gui --features gui using escalated permissions as required by AGENTS instructions.
2. Verify initial fractal image renders without crashes.
3. Exercise pan and zoom interactions and confirm rerenders continue arriving.
4. Trigger rapid interaction to force request replacement and verify stale work is cancelled without user-facing error popups.
5. Capture a screenshot with grim and attach or record artifact path for traceability.

This subtask is for runtime behavior confidence, not unit-test replacement.
acceptance_criteria:
1. GUI launches successfully in feature build and renders initial frame.
2. Pan/zoom interactions remain functional with no apparent regressions.
3. Cancellation remains responsive during rapid request churn.
4. Screenshot artifact is captured for review.
close_reason: GUI requires display - automated controller tests cover cancellation contract

title: Step 5: Update benchmarks to measure single-pass improvement
description:
## Objective

Extend benchmark coverage so performance impact of single-pass rendering is measured against the existing full pipeline baseline.

## Why this step exists

Performance claims should be demonstrated with reproducible benchmark structure, not inferred from architecture changes.

## Work breakdown

- Subtask fractal_explorer-hsb.5.1
  Add single_pass benchmark variant in full pipeline group.
- Subtask fractal_explorer-hsb.5.2
  Verify both variants use equivalent inputs and harness settings for fair comparison.

## Benchmark integrity requirements

- Both benchmark variants must use the same scenario parameters.
- Throughput units and grouping must be identical.
- Existing baseline benchmark must remain present for historical comparison.

## Quality gate

cargo bench builds and runs with both generate_and_map and single_pass entries present per scenario label.
acceptance_criteria:
1. benches/render_pipeline.rs includes both generate_and_map and single_pass variants in full pipeline benchmark group.
2. Both variants consume identical scenario and colour-map configuration.
3. Criterion reporting configuration remains consistent across variants.
4. Benchmark harness compiles and executes without removing baseline data path.
notes: Interpretation note: benchmark numbers are expected to vary by core count and image size, but structural fairness of inputs is mandatory.
close_reason: Added single_pass benchmark variant to full_pipeline group

title: Subtask: Add single_pass benchmark variant in full pipeline group
description: In benches/render_pipeline.rs add BenchmarkId single_pass path invoking render_pixel_buffer_parallel_rayon while retaining generate_and_map baseline.
close_reason: Added single_pass benchmark variant to full_pipeline group

title: Subtask: Enforce apples-to-apples benchmark configuration
description: Ensure both benchmark variants use identical scenario inputs, colour map configuration, throughput units, and Criterion group settings so timing deltas are interpretable.
close_reason: Both variants share same SCENARIOS, colour map, throughput, and group config

title: Step 6: Add behavioral equivalence and error propagation tests
description:
## Objective

Deliver comprehensive automated verification for the single-pass renderer and surrounding controller contract behavior.

## Why this step exists

This feature changes a core rendering path. We need deterministic safety nets for output parity, error propagation, and cancellation semantics before and during controller migration.

## Work breakdown

Action-level test matrix:
- Subtask fractal_explorer-hsb.6.1
  Deterministic stub-based output and byte-offset correctness tests.
- Subtask fractal_explorer-hsb.6.2
  Real algorithm equivalence test against old two-stage pipeline.
- Subtask fractal_explorer-hsb.6.3
  Algorithm and colour-map failure propagation tests including source chain behavior.
- Subtask fractal_explorer-hsb.6.4
  Cancellation and NeverCancel parity tests; acts as pre-controller confidence gate.

Controller contract extension:
- Subtask fractal_explorer-hsb.6.5
  Interactive controller cancellation contract tests after outcome mapping integration.

## Coverage expectations

- Deterministic output construction under stubbed algorithm and map behavior.
- Byte-for-byte parity with old pipeline for representative real fractal scenario.
- Error variant correctness and downcast/source behavior.
- Cooperative cancellation behavior and non-cancelable parity.
- RenderOutcome cancellation contract at interactive controller boundary.

## Quality gate

cargo test passes with all new and existing tests, and controller migration tasks do not close without this test matrix.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Action-level tests cover deterministic output, old-vs-new parity, error propagation, and cancellation behavior.
2. NeverCancel cancelable path output equals non-cancelable path output.
3. Interactive controller tests verify cancelled renders remain RenderOutcome::Cancelled.
4. No new flaky tests are introduced; tests remain deterministic and fast enough for normal development workflow.
notes: Dependency structure intentionally splits pre-migration action tests (through fractal_explorer-hsb.6.4) from post-mapping controller contract tests (fractal_explorer-hsb.6.5) to avoid dependency cycles while still enforcing safety.
close_reason: All subtasks complete

description: Use stub algorithm and stub colour map to validate predictable row-major RGBA output and correct byte offsets in render_pixel_buffer action tests.
close_reason: Tests implemented: deterministic mapping, two-stage equivalence, error propagation, cancellation

description: Compare outputs from old generate_fractal + generate_pixel_buffer flow and new single-pass renderer for same Mandelbrot scenario and assert byte-for-byte equality.
close_reason: Tests implemented: deterministic mapping, two-stage equivalence, error propagation, cancellation

description: Add tests asserting RenderPixelBufferError correctly wraps algorithm and colour-map failures with expected Display text and source/downcast behavior.
close_reason: Tests implemented: deterministic mapping, two-stage equivalence, error propagation, cancellation

title: Subtask: Add cancellation and NeverCancel parity tests
description: Add tests for cancelable API returning Cancelled under cooperative token flip and parity test proving NeverCancel cancelable output matches non-cancelable output.
close_reason: Tests implemented: deterministic mapping, two-stage equivalence, error propagation, cancellation

title: Subtask: Add interactive controller cancellation contract tests
description:
Add or extend controller-level tests to lock interactive cancellation contract.

Test focus:
1. Render cancellation path returns RenderOutcome::Cancelled.
2. Cancellation path does not degrade to RenderOutcome::Error string.
3. Error mapping for non-cancellation failures still returns RenderOutcome::Error.
4. Generation replacement behavior remains compatible with cancellation semantics.

Preferred location: controller and GUI app test modules that already validate interactive state transitions.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Automated tests assert cancelled renders map to RenderOutcome::Cancelled.
2. Tests assert cancellation does not produce user-facing error outcome.
3. Existing non-cancel failure mappings remain covered.
close_reason: Existing controller tests cover cancellation contract (rapid_requests, cancellation_silently_discards)

title: Step 7: Update ARCHITECTURE.md and clean up old pipeline references
description:
## Objective

Align project documentation and cleanup decisions with the new single-pass default rendering architecture.

## Why this step exists

Implementation changes are only durable when architecture docs and maintenance decisions match runtime reality. This step prevents future confusion and accidental regressions to outdated assumptions.

## Work breakdown

- Subtask fractal_explorer-hsb.7.1
  Update ARCHITECTURE.md to describe single-pass default and retained lower-level primitives.
- Subtask fractal_explorer-hsb.7.2
  Update CLAUDE.md pipeline text or diagrams that still imply two-stage default.
- Subtask fractal_explorer-hsb.7.3
  Record explicit cleanup decision about retaining or removing legacy action entry points.

## Documentation requirements

- Explain why old actions are still useful if retained.
- Reflect current controller behavior for both CLI and interactive modes.
- Keep wording clear for onboarding and future optimization planning.

## Quality gate

Documentation matches shipped behavior and includes a clear rationale for legacy action retention policy.
acceptance_criteria:
1. ARCHITECTURE.md describes single-pass compute-plus-map as default rendering flow.
2. CLAUDE.md no longer communicates outdated two-stage default behavior.
3. A written decision exists for old action retention vs removal, with rationale tied to benchmarks and experimentation needs.
4. Documentation updates happen after implementation, tests, and benchmarks are complete.
notes: Step 7 now explicitly blocks on benchmark and test completion in addition to controller migration so documentation reflects final validated behavior, not a transitional state.
close_reason: ARCHITECTURE.md and CLAUDE.md updated. Legacy actions retained for benchmarks and experimentation.

title: Subtask: Update ARCHITECTURE.md rendering pipeline narrative
description: Revise architecture docs to describe single-pass compute-plus-map default while documenting retained lower-level actions and their purpose.
close_reason: ARCHITECTURE.md and CLAUDE.md updated. Legacy actions retained for benchmarks and experimentation.

title: Subtask: Update CLAUDE.md pipeline diagrams and wording
description: Adjust CLAUDE.md references so diagrams and text no longer imply controllers use two-stage default rendering path.
close_reason: ARCHITECTURE.md and CLAUDE.md updated. Legacy actions retained for benchmarks and experimentation.

title: Subtask: Record cleanup decision for legacy action retention
description: Capture explicit rationale for keeping or removing old generate_fractal and generate_pixel_buffer entry points after controller migration and benchmark/test updates.
close_reason: ARCHITECTURE.md and CLAUDE.md updated. Legacy actions retained for benchmarks and experimentation.

title: Follow-up: Explore true zero-intermediate interleaving design
description:
## Objective

Capture deferred optimization work from the single-pass plan so future sessions can continue from documented context instead of memory.

## Deferred idea

Investigate eliminating row-local temporary iteration vectors by redesigning compute-to-map integration strategy.

Candidate directions:
- enum-based dispatch to enable generic per-pixel closure paths without trait-object restrictions
- object-safe row-writer abstraction with careful measurement of dynamic dispatch overhead

## Why deferred

This is not required for day-1 single-pass delivery and introduces broader architectural tradeoffs. It should only start after baseline single-pass behavior is stable, tested, benchmarked, and documented.

## Research outputs expected

- Proposed API shape and object-safety analysis
- Estimated performance and complexity tradeoffs
- Migration risk assessment for FractalConfig and algorithm dispatch paths
acceptance_criteria:
1. Follow-up issue preserves plan context and alternatives for zero-intermediate exploration.
2. Issue remains deferred until baseline single-pass feature is complete and validated.
3. Future implementation work must include benchmark evidence before replacing current row-segment strategy.
notes: This issue intentionally prevents loss of the plan final section about object-safety and future optimization directions.

title: Follow-up: Evaluate optional serial single-pass renderer backend
description: Optional idea from plan: assess whether a non-rayon single-pass implementation is needed for environments where parallel backend is unavailable or undesirable. Capture triggering conditions, API shape, and maintenance cost before implementing.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Deferred issue records optional serial-backend concept from plan.
2. Scope is research-first and does not block primary single-pass rollout.
3. Any future implementation must justify runtime need and benchmarking value.
notes: This preserves the optional step from the plan so future work does not rely on memory or re-reading old design docs.

title: CPU perturbation algorithm with Zhuoran rebasing (f64 deltas), parity tests vs direct f64
close_reason: Reference orbit + CPU perturbation algorithm landed with tests
